data.title: CareDx rebounds as draft LCD suggests no change in coverage
data.description:
Following a ~38% sell-off in the previous session, CareDx (NASDAQ:CDNA) traded higher on Friday after the company said it doesn’t expect the use of its testing products to change in response to the newly released draft Local Coverage Determination (LCD) related to organ transplants.

The Brisbane, California-based diagnostic product maker markets various testing services designed to detect the presence or absence of organ rejection in pre- and post-transplant settings.

Citing a proposed draft LCD related to molecular testing for solid organ allograft rejection, CareDx (NASDAQ:CDNA) said there is no change in Medicare coverage currently offered for its products.

“We do not expect changes in the use of our testing in response to this draft LCD and remain deeply committed to patient care and providing access to our testing,” CEO John Hanna remarked.

However, CareDx (NASDAQ:CDNA) noted that the draft policy suggests a bundled payment system for surveillance testing. The company expects to share additional details during the earnings call scheduled for Aug. 6, as further reviews on the LCD are ongoing. The draft policy is open for public comments until Aug. 31.

data.title: Upexi's cryptocurrency strategy pays off with 67% weekly gain
data.description:
Shares of Upexi (NASDAQ:UPXI) closed out the week with a significant gain, thanks to the company’s increased investment in Solana (SOL) cryptocurrency.

Last Friday, Upexi (NASDAQ:UPXI) had secured $50M through an equity offering and $150M through a note offering with investors, earmarking the funds for business operations, working capital, and funding the company’s Solana treasury strategy. The notes are backed by Upexi’s (NASDAQ:UPXI) holdings in Solana.

Upon closing of the note and equity offering, Upexi’s Solana treasury will more than double to ~1.65M SOL. According to data from Coinbase (COIN), the value of SOL increased 11% to ~$182, raising the value of Upexi’s (UPXI) holdings to more than $300M.

Tampa-based Upexi (UPXI), which manufactures, develops, and distributes consumer products on marketplace platforms like Amazon (AMZN), has been actively building its holding in cryptocurrencies to capitalize on the growth opportunities in digital currencies.

“We will continue to aggressively accumulate SOL in a disciplined manner for the benefit of our shareholders,” Upexi CEO Allan Marshall said in the company’s recent quarterly results.

The strategy has contributed to a 254% gain in Upexi’s (UPXI) share price since the beginning of July. By comparison, in its latest quarterly earnings report, Upexi (UPXI) reported a loss of $3.83M, or $(2.87) per share.

Shares are up 20% on Friday, adding to the stock’s 67% gain for the week ending July 18.

data.title: Amphenol appears on Evercore's 'Tactical Outperform' list ahead of Q2 earnings
data.description:
Amphenol (NYSE:APH) was added to Evercore ISI's "Tactical Outperform" list ahead of its second quarter 2025 financial results due in part to its ability to navigate tariff and macroeconomic concerns.

"We think APH should report upside vs. street estimates for the Jun-qtr given higher AI ramps (NVDA commentary on NVL72 shipments), pockets of recovery in Industrials (although parts of Europe remains challenged), and continual strength in traditional IT/Datacom and Mil/Aero," said Evercore analysts, led by Amit Daryanani, in a Friday investor note. 

Evercore also bumped up its price target on the stock to $110 from $105.

"Consensus revenue estimate for Sept-qtr is lower than historical seasonality (+3.1% vs. +6.4% historically), and we think there is potential for APH to guide to better revenues and EPS," Daryanani added.

Amphenol is slated to release its second quarter results at 1 p.m. ET on Wednesday, July 23. A consensus estimate calls for earnings per share of $0.67 on revenue of $5.04B. Last quarter the company beat the adjusted EPS estimate by $0.11 and the revenue estimate by more than half a billion dollars. In fact, Amphenol has surpassed the revenue estimate every quarter since at least the second quarter of 2020.

Amphenol shares ticked up 1% during Friday market action. The stock has surged nearly 50% in 2025.

data.title: Comerica outlines improved 2025 loan outlook and plans $100M Q3 share repurchase amid robust loan growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Comerica Incorporated (CMA) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Curtis Chatman Farmer emphasized, "We are incredibly proud of this quarter's results. We saw an inflection in loans as balances grew consistently throughout the quarter across most of our businesses. While deposits came down modestly, favorable loan, fee income and expense trends drove a sizable increase in both net income and PPNR." Farmer also highlighted, "Capitalization remained a strength with an estimated CET1 of 11.94%, well above our 10% strategic target, even after a compelling dividend and higher share repurchases."
  • Farmer noted, "Beyond our financial results, this was an exciting quarter for payments and deposits as we announce new capabilities and product enhancements for our customers."
  • CFO James J. Herzog stated, "Net interest income remained stable at $575 million for the third consecutive quarter as higher loans offset the impact of deposits. The lower benefit from BSBY cessation was effectively offset by one more day in the quarter."
  • Herzog added, "Our strong capital position afforded us the opportunity to redeem preferred stock, avoiding a more punitive coupon reset, but also resulting in a slight negative drag to EPS this quarter from costs related to the preferred stock redemption."

Outlook

  • Comerica now projects full year 2025 average loans to be flat to down 1%, representing an improvement from prior guidance. Herzog reported, "Second quarter results exceeded expectations and pipelines and activity levels remain strong, supporting our outlook for consistent growth in the third and fourth quarters."
  • The company expects full year average deposits down 2% to 3% in 2025, with a deliberate reduction in brokered CDs and ongoing strength in core deposit growth.
  • Herzog stated, "We still project net interest income growth of 5% to 7% in 2025," while noting that third quarter net interest income may decline slightly relative to the second quarter, which could result in full year results at the lower end of that range.
  • Comerica plans to repurchase approximately $100 million of common stock in the third quarter.
  • The outlook for full year 2025 noninterest expenses improved, with a new projection of only 2% growth year-over-year, citing strong expense performance year-to-date.

Financial Results

  • Comerica reported earnings per share of $1.42, an almost 14% increase over the prior quarter.
  • Loans grew throughout the quarter, with average loans up almost 1% and period-end loans up approximately 3%. Total commitments increased by $400 million, according to Herzog.
  • Average deposits declined just over 1%, with noninterest-bearing deposits remaining flat at 38% of total deposits for the fourth consecutive quarter.
  • Noninterest income increased $20 million, capital markets income improved $11 million, and expenses decreased $23 million over the prior quarter, driven by lower litigation, salaries, and benefits.
  • Comerica returned $193 million to common shareholders through share repurchases and dividends in the quarter.
  • CET1 ratio was reported at 11.94%.

Q&A

  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the NII trajectory and the expected decline in Q3 before an upturn in Q4. Herzog replied, "We do expect a slight decrease in Q3 before continuing our network trajectory in Q4... we do have some particular events occurring in the third quarter... The first of these is the redemption of our preferred stock... The second is pay rates on deposits... pay rates will likely step up in the third quarter... at least twice the increase of the 4 bps that you saw in Q2."
  • Gosalia also asked about expenses. Herzog responded, "Let's keep in mind that we did have an incredible second quarter on expenses... some project expense was deferred from the second quarter to the second half of the year."
  • Jon Glenn Arfstrom, RBC: Asked about loan growth pipeline and long-term potential. Peter L. Sefzik said, "Our manager surveys came in more positive... pipelines grew, and we're feeling pretty good about the second half of the year."
  • Arfstrom inquired about achieving the higher end of NII guidance. Herzog said, "Noninterest-bearing deposits is still very much an x factor."
  • David Alan George, Baird: Pressed on long-term shareholder value and efficiency. Farmer responded, "We are always focused on improving performance across the company... we're going to lean into those things on a go-forward basis."
  • Bernard Von Gizycki, Deutsche Bank: Asked about M&A thresholds and deposit seasonality. Farmer said, "The $100 billion threshold for us is not a governor as to whether we would look at a transaction or not."
  • Michael Lawrence Mayo, Wells Fargo: Challenged management on independence and performance. Farmer answered, "We are always aware of the need to perform at an acceptable level... we are always going to do the right thing by our shareholders."
  • Anthony Albert Elian, JPMorgan: Queried on deposit pricing and payment products. Herzog and Sefzik detailed the competitive environment and noted, "It's still a little bit to be determined, and I think that we are monitoring the situation."
  • Andrew Steven Leischner, KBW: Asked about deposit pricing and credit. Herzog noted ongoing competitive pressure in deposit pricing, while Melinda A. Chausse provided color on increased criticized loans, emphasizing concentration in three consumer-related credits.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed persistent skepticism on long-term shareholder value, efficiency, and independence, with several pressing for clarity on performance improvements and strategic alternatives, indicating a slightly negative tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and sometimes defensive tone, repeatedly emphasizing improved results, structural tailwinds, and ongoing investments, while acknowledging past underperformance and current challenges. Farmer stated, "We are incredibly proud of this quarter's results," and, "We are always aware of the need to perform at an acceptable level."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone in prepared remarks was more upbeat, while Q&A exchanges continued to see probing from analysts and defensive posture from executives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for average loans improved from down 1% to 2% to flat to down 1%, showing increased optimism.
  • The company maintained its net interest income growth projection of 5% to 7%, but now expects results to potentially be at the lower end of the range due to deposit trends and preferred stock redemption.
  • Expense growth guidance improved to 2% from a prior 2% to 3%. Capital return increased, with $193 million returned in Q2 compared to $143 million in Q1.
  • Analyst focus shifted further toward long-term performance, efficiency, and strategic alternatives, with more pointed questions on shareholder value and the company’s independence.
  • Management’s confidence increased in prepared remarks, highlighting positive momentum, but defensiveness remained during analyst exchanges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty and persistent inflation and elevated rates impacting customer profitability.
  • Increased criticized loans were concentrated in credits with consumer exposure (luxury goods, liquor industry, transportation), with Chausse noting, "All of them are pressured by this longer -- higher-for-longer rate environment."
  • Deposit pricing is expected to remain competitive, with Herzog stating, "We intend to remain diligent and agile with our pricing strategy as we monitor the competitive environment."
  • Analysts raised concerns about efficiency ratio, long-term shareholder value, and competitive positioning in the context of ongoing industry consolidation.

Final Takeaway

Comerica’s management underscored improved loan trends, stable credit quality, and ongoing investments in payments and deposits as key drivers behind stronger profitability and capital returns in the second quarter. The company updated its guidance to reflect a more optimistic loan outlook for 2025, maintained net interest income growth expectations, and signaled continued discipline in expense management. While analysts pressed for greater clarity on long-term shareholder value and efficiency, management reiterated its commitment to responsible growth, robust capital levels, and strategic deployment of capital through share repurchases and dividends, positioning Comerica to capitalize on emerging opportunities amid a competitive and uncertain environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: HHS sued by state attorneys general over ACA rule they say creates coverage barriers
data.description:
A group of state attorneys general has sued HHS over a proposed rule set to go into effect that they claim would lead to up to 1.8 million individuals losing health insurance coverage through Affordable Care Act marketplaces.

The 20 state attorneys general, along with Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (Dem.), say that a Trump administration final rule would make changes to state and federal insurance marketplaces that would lead to higher premiums and out-of-pocket costs.

Those changes also include additional verification requirements, an automatic monthly charge on automatically reenrolled consumers who qualify for $0 premiums, and narrowing the open enrollment period.

They add that the rule would transfer more health care costs to states.

The lawsuit, filed in a Massachusetts federal court, argues that the rule is "arbitrary and capricious, contrary to law, and violates the Administrative Procedure Act," according to a news release from New Jersey Attorney General Matthew Platkin.

The attorneys general are asking the court for preliminary relief, as well as a stay to block provisions of the rule from taking effect before the effective date of August 25.

Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV), UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH), CVS Health's Aetna (NYSE:CVS), Centene (NYSE:CNC) and Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR) are among the largest insurers in ACA marketplaces.

data.title: Diana Shipping becomes one of largest Genco shareholders after acquiring stake
data.description:
Genco Shipping (NYSE:GNK) +2.1% in Friday's trading after disclosing it took a 7.7% equity stake in Diana Shipping (NYSE:DSX), making it one of Diana's largest shareholders alongside BlackRock and Berge Bulk.

A 13-D filing with SEC - used when an investor does not intend to remain passive - showed Diana (NYSE:DSX) has acquired ~3.3M shares in Genco (NYSE:GNK), valued at ~$46M.

Genco (GNK) operates a fleet of more than 40 vessels ranging from supramaxes to capesizes, with a total carrying capacity of 4.44M dwt, while Diana's (DSX) current fleet totals 36 ships from newcastlemaxes and ultramaxes, with 4.1M dwt on the water.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares lifts NII growth guide after Q2 earnings slightly beat
data.description:
Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN) on Friday raised its full-year guidance for net interest income growth after Q2 2025 headline numbers came in mixed, HBAN shares dipped 2.4% in late morning trading.

The regional lender now expects full-year NII to advance 8%-9% (midpoint 8.5%) from the 2024 baseline of $5.40B, up from 5%-7% (midpoint 6%) in the previous view released in April.

It sees average loans rising 6%-8% (midpoint 7%) from $124.5B last year, compared with the previous outlook o 5%-7% (midpoint 6%). Average deposits are expected to drift up 4%-6% (midpoint 5%) from $155.1B in 2024, vs. 3%-5% (midpoint 4%) in the prior goal.

Meanwhile, Q2 adjusted EPS of $0.34, a hair above the $0.33 average analyst estimate, stalled from Q1 and rose from $0.28 in Q1 2024. The latest figure includes a $0.04 impact resulting from a $58M decrease in pretax earnings from a securities repositioning and notable items that lowered pretax earnings by $3M.

Q2 revenue (fully taxable equivalent) of $1.95B, trailing the $1.98B expected, increased from $1.94B in Q1 and $1.82B in Q2 2024.

Net interest income ("FTE") rose to $1.48B in Q2 from $1.44B in Q1 and $1.33B a year before.

Noninterest income decreased 5% sequentially and 4% Y/Y to $471M.

Average total deposits drifted up to $163.4B in Q2 from $161.6B in Q1 Total deposit costs inched down to 2.02% in Q2 from 2.03% in Q1.

Average loans and leases were $133.1B in Q2 vs. $130.9B in Q1.

data.title: Signs of labor market weakness support a rate cut, Fed's Waller says
data.description:
The time may be running out for the Federal Reserve's "wait-and-see" approach on monetary policy, Federal Reserve Governor Chris Waller said in a broadcast interview aired on Friday. He has said it's time for the Federal Open Market Committee to cut its rate, currently at 4.25%-4.50%.

He indicated that he might dissent at the July meeting if his colleagues vote to keep rates unchanged at the next meeting, although he feels it's important to not dissent too often. 

Labor market data is showing signs of stress, Waller said. "The headline numbers for the labor market, what we're seeing, are okay. But when you get underneath and start looking at the data, the private sector is not doing as well as everybody thinks it is," Waller said in an interview on Bloomberg Television.

About half of the U.S.'s employment growth last month came from the public sector. "That means the private sector isn't doing particularly well," he said.

He likened the situation to walking on ice and starting to hear cracking sounds.  "When you start hearing cracks — and that's what I feel like — it's too late once you go through the ice. So you've got to start prepping in advance," he said.

Currently, there's "not much happening" in the labor market as businesses stay in a holding pattern — they're not hiring, but they're not firing, either. "It wouldn't take much to just sort of tip it," Waller said.

"Could I be wrong? Absolutely," he said. For example, last year the labor market showed weakness for a couple of months, then rebounded.

But recent data, including JOLTS and the Fed's Beige Book, "are not indicating a super-healthy private labor market," Waller said.

While the Fed governor is willing to cut rates, traders don't think that's likely to happen in July. The probability of a 25 basis-point cut at the July 29-30 meeting stands at only 4.7%, with a 95.3% chance that the rate will stay unchanged, according to the CME FedWatch tool. The odds rise to 57.9% for a 25-bp cut at the September meeting.

Waller has been mentioned as a candidate to replace Jerome Powell, whose term as Fed chair ends in May 2026. Recent reporting suggests that National Economic Council Chair Kevin Hassett has an edge in securing President Donald Trump's nomination.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Publishing giant McGraw Hill eyes U.S. IPO more than a decade after going private
data.description:
U.S. textbook publisher and educational materials company McGraw Hill Inc. is making a return to the public markets after a 13-year break.

The company, which was separated from The McGraw-Hill Companies and taken private back in 2012 in a $2.5B transaction by Apollo Global Management (APO), is now offering 24.4M shares of common stock between $19 and $22 per share in an initial public offering to raise up to $536.6M.

It is looking to list itself on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol "MH" and is targeting a market cap value of over $4B.

Selling stockholders of the company have also granted the underwriters of the IPO an option to purchase up to 3.7M additional shares solely to cover over-allotments. McGraw Hill will have over 191M shares outstanding after going public.

In a Securities and Exchange Commission filing from Thursday, the company disclosed that California-based investment firm Platinum Equity will continue to own 86.5% of the voting power once the IPO is concluded, making it a “controlled company.” The asset manager bought McGraw Hill from Apollo in 2021 for $4.5B. 

The company expects to use the proceeds from the IPO to repay about $466.6M of outstanding borrowings under its A&E term loan. Upon going public, the company does not plan to pay dividends to any common stockholders.

For the fiscal year that ended on March 31, 2025, the company reported a 7% jump in total revenue at $2.1B, and its net loss shrank 55.5% to $85.8M. About 65% of the total revenue came from its digital segment, where revenue was up 8% at $1.4B.

For the three months ended June 30, 2025, the company expects revenue between $528.2M and $536.2M; net income in the range of $477M and $527M; adjusted net income of $290M and $296M; and adjusted EBITDA of $184.9M and $191.9M.

In the IPO filing, the company warned that generative AI systems like ChatGPT may make it easier for educational materials to be created, which could negatively affect demand for its products and also its pricing, among other risks.

"Access to our copyrighted content, including through third-party, so-called “pirate sites,” may also contribute to such generative AI training efforts. It is therefore possible that our copyrighted content or proprietary data may be ingested or scraped by, and used to train such AI systems without our knowledge or consent," the company said in the filing.

"It is also possible that one of our competitors, or other providers of data sources relevant to our products, could make a deal to train generative AI models on their data, which could allow the generative AI model to create textbooks and other learning materials competitive with ours," the company added.

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC (GS), BMO Capital Markets (BMO), J.P. Morgan (JPM), Macquarie Capital, Morgan Stanley (MS), Deutsche Bank Securities (DB), UBS Investment Bank (UBS), Baird, BTIG, Needham & Company, Rothschild & Co., Stifel (SF), and William Blair are among the underwriters to the IPO.

More on IPOs

  • Bit Digital subsidiary proposes IPO
  • Figma files for IPO, set to trade under FIG ticker
  • Electric bike startup Lime preps for its long-awaited IPO

data.title: Alliant Energy declares $0.5075 dividend
data.description:
• Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) declared $0.5075/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.16%
  • Payable Aug. 15; for shareholders of record July 31; ex-div July 31.
